title: Signal Tanker Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week: Clean Diesel Oil Flows Russia to all destinations
Russian diesel oil exports in September hit the lowest level of the year
In the last week of September, there is some optimism in the VLCC crude oil freight market after a difficult summer season. It should be noted, however, that while there are positive signs, growth rates in tonne days aren't yet robust. The real test is ahead as we move into October, with supply figures already showing a downward trend.

children.title: Chart of the Week: Clean Diesel Oil Flows Russia to all destinations
section_path: Chart of the Week: Clean Diesel Oil Flows Russia to all destinations
children.section_path_text: Chart of the Week: Clean Diesel Oil Flows Russia to all destinations
children.summary:
Russian diesel oil exports in September hit the lowest level of the year
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-28T130123.172.png | assets/2023-09-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-28t130123172_3ff9cff24881.png
In the last week of September, there is some optimism in the VLCC crude oil freight market after a difficult summer season. It should be noted, however, that while there are positive signs, growth rates in tonne days aren't yet robust.

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT
children.summary:
Market Rates (WS)
‘Dirty' WS VLCC - Suezmax - Aframax Mixed
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-28T131153.572.png | assets/2023-09-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-28t131153572_7a65bcbb6ec4.png
The crude freight sentiment appears to be recovering for the second week in a row on the VLCC MEG -China route, while stable levels are being maintained on the Suezmax Wafr-Cont route, and stronger momentum is being seen on the Aframax-Med route. VLCC MEG-China freight rates rose by 20% from the previous week, WS 51, with signs for a further rise at the end of the month
Suezmax freight rates for shipments from West Africa to continental Europe recorded a downward revision to WS70, down 10% from a month ago, while rates on the Suez-Baltic-Med route remained unchanged at WS72 for three consecutive weeks.

children.title: ‘Product' WS LR2 Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-28T131237.700.png | assets/2023-09-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-28t131237700_ff54cd6b6aa5.png
LR2 AG freight rates fell to 125 WS, down 7% week-on-week, while a weaker trend appears to be emerging for the end of the month. Panamax Weaker
LR2 AG freight rates fell to 125 WS, down 7% week-on-week, while a weaker trend appears to be emerging for the end of the month.

children.title: Panamax Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-28T131341.050.png | assets/2023-09-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-28t131341050_a7e2a87c0f5d.png
Panamax Carib-to-USG rates fell to 131 WS, down 60% from a year ago, with the downward trend continuing since the end of Week 27.

children.title: ‘Clean' MR Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-28T131501.789.png | assets/2023-09-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-28t131501789_4cbd3d78d839.png
MR1 rates for the Baltic continent increased to WS 200, up 12% from the previous week, but rates are still well below those of a year ago, up 45%. MR2 rates for shipments from the continent to the U.S.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY
children.summary:
Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
'Dirty' (#vessels) - Decreasing
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-28T131554.367.png | assets/2023-09-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-28t131554367_87d9545e8936.png
Crude oil tanker supply showed the first signs of decline, with the Ras Tanuara VLCC number below the annual average, while the Aframax number approached the annual average, with a tendency to fall to a lower level by the end of the month. VLCC Ras Tanura : The current number of ships is 52, almost 10 below the annual average, and intense volatility over the last two weeks.

children.title: 'Clean' LR2 (#vessels) - Increasing
children.summary: Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-28T131709.021.png | assets/2023-09-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-28t131709021_6d6918a9c62a.png

children.title: MR1 (#vessels) - Increasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-28T132058.230.png | assets/2023-09-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-28t132058230_945ead4bb45a.png
Clean LR2 AG Jubail: The number of ships sustained the increase of the third week of September, and rose further to 9 ships, 5 above the annual average. Clean MR1 Algeria Skikda : The current count is 29 ships, 10 more than a week ago, while the number is still below the annual average of 33.

children.title: ‘Dirty' Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-28T132448.264.png | assets/2023-09-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-28t132448264_9dadf6c567ae.png
Dirty tonne days: At the end of September, the percentage increase in demand (in tonne days) was down for all crude oil vessel categories; despite signs of an upturn in Aframax, there was eventually a downward correction. In the VLCC segment, growth rates continued to be much steeper than eight weeks earlier.

children.title: ‘Clean' Panamax / MR Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-28T132538.173.png | assets/2023-09-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-28t132538173_6025ba74dd29.png
Panamax tonne days: The growth rate has steadily increased over the last two weeks, with the last peak recorded in week 36. Clean MR tonne days: Downward pressure continues to be revised sharply downward for the MR1 size, while demand sentiment for MR2 appears to be gradually increasing toward the end of September.
